author: terencechain
content:
Dora says:
```
[buildoor] time="2026-06-14T02:35:47Z" level=info msg="Head event received" component=epbs-service root=cf7fba2649218e5f slot=94
[dora] time="2026-06-14T02:35:47Z" level=info msg="received block 2:94 [0xcf7fba2649218e5f22bc2ad6a7494103683cb772cebdc40d9e026a7b09eeeb68] stream  (5 ms, 0 ms, 0 ms) fork: 2" client=1-nethermind-lodestar service=cl-indexer
[dora] time="2026-06-14T02:35:47Z" level=error msg="failed processing execution payload 94 (0xcf7fba2649218e5f22bc2ad6a7494103683cb772cebdc40d9e026a7b09eeeb68): GET failed with status 404: {\"message\":\"execution payload envelope not found\",\"code\":404}" client=2-nethermind-prysm service=cl-indexer
[dora] time="2026-06-14T02:35:47Z" level=error msg="failed processing execution payload 94 (0xcf7fba2649218e5f22bc2ad6a7494103683cb772cebdc40d9e026a7b09eeeb68): GET failed with status 404: {\"code\":404,\"message\":\"Route GET:/eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_envelope/0xcf7fba2649218e5f22bc2ad6a7494103683cb772cebdc40d9e026a7b09eeeb68 not found\"}" client=1-nethermind-lodestar service=cl-indexer
```
on both prysm and lodestar

author: terencechain
content:
<@808969530608451584> i think there's a race? since we dont self build anymore the payload can come later. Cluade:
```
dora 404s on GET /eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_envelope/{root} every slot. It streams the block and immediately asks for the envelope, but the envelope is a separate gossip
  object that lands ~200-300ms later, so it's not in the db yet when dora queries. We don't save it until ReceiveExecutionPayloadEnvelope runs. dora doesn't retry, so it just logs
  the miss.
```

author: pk910
content: dora only tries fetching the payload once it receives the execution_payload_available event

author: terencechain
content:
hmm sorry they said it wrong, but we might have a bug anyway. There's still a race here: 

we emit `execution_payload_available`. when both payload and blobs are seen (before we save it to the database)

we emit `execution_payload`.   after everything is procseed (in database)

if you use `execution_payload`.. then it should work.. but im not sure which event is for what anymore

author: nflaig
content: dora needs to use `execution_payload`, left some more details here https://github.com/ethereum/beacon-APIs/pull/588, I believe the reason dora was using `execution_payload_available` before is because most clients didn't have #588 implemented? but anyways, only consumer of `execution_payload_available` should be ptc and maybe some monitoring tooling
